steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The blank connects two findings from the same brain-imaging study: actors showed suppressed brain responses during performances (Sentence 1), and actors showed normal responses outside of performances (Sentence 2). The decisive clue is that the two results are direct opposites — suppressed vs. normal, in-performance vs. out-of-performance — so the transition must signal contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — specifically,: "Specifically" signals that the second idea narrows or elaborates on the first. Sentence 2 does not narrow Sentence 1; it presents an opposite result in a different context. ✗
  • Choice B — likewise,: "Likewise" signals similarity. The two findings are opposite (suppressed vs. normal), so a similarity transition contradicts the relationship. ✗
  • Choice C — thus,: "Thus" signals cause and effect, meaning Sentence 2 results from Sentence 1. Sentence 2 is a parallel finding in a different context, not a consequence of Sentence 1. ✗
  • Choice D — conversely,: "Conversely" signals a reversal or opposite. This exactly matches the relationship: suppressed responses during acting vs. normal responses outside acting. ✓
That matches Choice D — conversely,.
